bertscore.original_cuda_error:
CUDA error: no kernel image is available for execution on the device
Search for `cudaErrorNoKernelImageForDevice' in https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__TYPES.html for more information.
CUDA kernel errors might be asynchronously reported at some other API call, so the stacktrace below might be incorrect.
For debugging consider passing CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1
Compile with `TORCH_USE_CUDA_DSA` to enable device-side assertions.

per_entity_results.generated: Network Latency and Energy Consumption Measurements with a Load Generation Workload
per_entity_results.reference: MoonGen Load Latency Benchmarks

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset documents an experiment measuring network latency and energy consumption during workload execution. A load generation tool, MoonGen, was configured and used to transmit packets between nodes at varying rates and sizes. The methodology involved synchronizing nodes, recording energy measurements, executing the load generator, and capturing latency data as a histogram. Per-run logs and evaluation outputs are included alongside the collected measurement artifacts.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment measures l2-load-latency in a two-node setup with MoonGen on one node as the load generator and libmoon on another node as the forwarding device under test. The benchmark tests different packet rates and packet sizes. Each measurement runs for fixed duration, records energy consumption on both machines, and stores latency histograms as CSV data and PNG plots for every parameter combination.

per_entity_results.generated: The script configures a device under test by cloning a Git repository, checking out a specific commit, building software, and enabling hugetlbfs. It establishes the system state for subsequent measurements by disabling CPU turbo boost via a kernel interface. Hostname and Git commit identifiers are retrieved from environment variables during execution.
per_entity_results.reference: Prepares the DUT node by building libmoon and enabling hugepage support. It also disables CPU turbo boost for more stable forwarding measurements.

per_entity_results.generated: The script configures and builds a load generation tool, MoonGen, from a Git repository to prepare for workload execution. It retrieves configuration variables for the repository location and commit identifier. The script also installs build dependencies and sets up hugetlbfs before completing its initialization phase.
per_entity_results.reference: Prepares the load-generator node by building MoonGen from the provided repository and branch. Additionally, it enables hugepage support for packet generation and latency measurements.

per_entity_results.generated: File copy from /home/warmuth/experiments/experiments/loop-experiment-llm/evaluation/plot_latency.py to MoonGen/plot_latency.py on node cesis.
per_entity_results.reference: File copy from /home/warmuth/experiments/experiments/loop-experiment-llm/evaluation/plot_latency.py to MoonGen/plot_latency.py on node cesis.

per_entity_results.generated: The command executes a load generation program to measure latency under specified conditions and record results.
per_entity_results.reference: Runs MoonGen's l2-load-latency example using the selected transmit and receive ports and histogram output paths.

per_entity_results.generated: This script configures and executes a network load generation workload, capturing latency measurements during operation. It synchronizes with other nodes, initiates energy measurement recording, runs the load generator process, and generates a histogram plot of the captured latencies. The resulting data files are uploaded for subsequent analysis.
per_entity_results.reference: Looped benchmark that iterates over all configured packet-rate and packet-size combinations, synchronizes with the DUT, records node energy usage and runs MoonGen's l2-load-latency example. Afterwards it uploads the generated latency histogram and plot.

per_entity_results.generated: This script executes a network load generation benchmark, varying packet rate and size to measure latency. Per-run output includes a histogram of latencies saved as a comma separated value file and an associated plot image. The recorded data facilitates evaluation of the system's performance under different traffic conditions.
per_entity_results.reference: Each iteration runs the MoonGen l2-load-latency script with different values for packet rate and packet size, storing the measured latencies in a CSV file, generating a plot, and uploading both artifacts after each run.

per_entity_results.generated: The command executes a simulation with specified parameters to measure latency between two network nodes.
per_entity_results.reference: Runs libmoon's l2-forward example so the DUT forwards traffic during the load-generator measurement window.

per_entity_results.generated: This script initiates and synchronizes a latency measurement workload within the libmoon environment, capturing energy consumption per run. It transmits packets between specified ports at a defined rate and size, then terminates the process and uploads associated logs. The script facilitates automated testing and performance evaluation of the forwarding functionality.
per_entity_results.reference: Forwarder that synchronizes with the load-generator node, records energy usage and forwards the received packets back to the load-generator node.

per_entity_results.generated: The script executes a layer-2 forwarding benchmark, varying packet rate and size to measure energy consumption during transmission. Per-run logs record the performance of the forwarding action under different parameter combinations. The primary output is power usage data associated with each configuration.
per_entity_results.reference: Each iteration runs the libmoon l2-forward script to forward the received packets from the loadgen node.
